region_of_origin: United States (served in Singapore by Old Chang Kee)
food_subgroup: Fried potato snacks with cheese sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term exists for tater tots with nacho cheese sauce. FOODON_03301710 (potato product) used as closest parent. Nacho cheese sauce component is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Potato products and dishes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for tater tots with nacho cheese sauce as a composite dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept identified for tater tots with nacho cheese sauce.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup serving of tater tots with nacho cheese sauce, as sold by Old Chang Kee, weighs approximately 85 grams and contains roughly 10–12 individual potato tot pieces accompanied by nacho cheese dipping sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Potato puffs, frozen, oven-heated (Tater Tots) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; Cheese sauce, ready-to-serve — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for (1) frozen potato puffs/tater tots (FDC ID 170481, oven-heated) and (2) ready-to-serve cheese sauce (FDC ID 173580). The composite per-100g values are weighted at approximately 75% tater tots and 25% nacho cheese sauce, reflecting a typical cup-serve ratio. Old Chang Kee does not publish detailed nutrition data; no HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this specific product. USDA SR Legacy is the highest-priority approved source available for this Western fast-food snack.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; Potato products GI reference data
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tater tots with nacho cheese sauce. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for french fries / fried potato products (GI range 63–75, mean ~65, medium category). The cheese sauce component contributes fat and protein which moderately lower the overall GI relative to plain fried potato. GL per serving calculated as: (65 × 22.5 g available carbohydrate per 85 g serving) / 100 = 14.6. Available carbohydrate = carbohydrate_g per 100g (26.5) × 0.85 kg serving fraction = 22.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~390 mg per 100 g; ~332 mg per 85 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid tater tots with nacho cheese sauce due to the high sodium load from both the seasoned potato component and the processed cheese sauce, which together can contribute over 14% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~65) and moderate glycemic load (~14.6 per serving) from refined starch in fried potato
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume tater tots with nacho cheese sauce only occasionally and in small portions, as the medium GI and moderate GL can cause a meaningful postprandial blood glucose rise; pairing with a protein or fibre source may help blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~3.1 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g per 100 g), high sodium, and ultra-processed food classification
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of tater tots with nacho cheese sauce, as the combination of saturated fat, residual trans fat, high sodium, and ultra-processed ingredients is associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tater Tots with Nacho Cheese is a Western-origin fast-food snack sold in Singapore by Old Chang Kee under the description 'Mini Hashbrown comes in a cup.' The dish consists of small, cylindrical deep-fried potato bites — commonly known as tater tots — made from shredded or riced potato that is seasoned, formed into compact nugget shapes, and fried until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft and starchy inside. The tots are served in a cup alongside or drizzled with a warm nacho-style processed cheese sauce, which is creamy, mildly tangy, and savoury. The overall flavour profile is salty, rich, and indulgent, with a satisfying contrast between the crunchy potato exterior and the smooth, viscous cheese sauce. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate fat content (~11.8 g per 100 g) and a significant carbohydrate contribution (~26.5 g per 100 g) primarily from potato starch. Sodium is notably high (~390 mg per 100 g), making this a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), and a standard 85 g cup serving delivers a glycaemic load of approximately 14.6, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is classified as ultra-processed and contains common allergens including milk (from the cheese sauce), wheat, and soy. It is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans, and halal status is not confirmed for the Old Chang Kee version. This snack is popular as a casual street-food-style treat and is typically consumed as a standalone snack or light meal accompaniment.

Mini hashbrown cup Old Chang Kee nutrition
Are tater tots with nacho cheese vegetarian?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific product. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for frozen tater tots and ready-to-serve cheese sauce, weighted by approximate serving composition. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for fried potato products. Old Chang Kee does not publish detailed nutritional information publicly. Medium confidence reflects the use of component-level estimation rather than a direct product analysis.
notes: 1. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 2. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and carry moderate uncertainty. 3. The input record incorrectly classified this food under cuisine 'Curry Puff' and food_group 'Dairy and eggs'; these have been corrected to 'American / Fast Food Snack' and 'Snacks' respectively, as tater tots with nacho cheese is a Western-origin fried potato snack, not a curry puff. 4. Halal status is listed as false/unconfirmed because Old Chang Kee's halal certification status for this specific product line could not be verified from approved sources at time of processing. 5. The serving size of 85 g is an estimate based on typical fast-food cup-serve portions for this category; no official weight is published by Old Chang Kee. 6. All -1 sentinel values indicate nutrients not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data used for this composite estimate.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Curry Puff' to 'American / Fast Food Snack' — input was erroneous.
Corrected food_group from 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Snacks' — input was erroneous.
Added mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this food is served in Singapore.
Built full nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy using composite weighting (75% tater tots, 25% cheese sauce).
Added GI estimate of 65 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous fried potato data with full documentation.
Added all three required health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.

Built comprehensive aliases list and aliases_by_language. Specifically, added 'Nai Lao Shu Qiu (Chinese romanized)' to the flat 'aliases' list to resolve the consistency issue identified in the previous iteration's Q8 failure.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes field (vitamin_d_mcg and others not reported in source).
Verified zero-macro justification rule not triggered — all core macros are non-zero.
Set ontology match types to only allowed values: 'closest_parent' and 'none'.
